description: Optimizing a kernel before locking a correctness baseline, so a faster-but-wrong kernel is reported as an improvement.
body:
Failure mode: the kernel is optimized (shared-memory staging, vectorized loads, padding) and the benchmark shows a speedup, but no reference-comparison baseline was captured first, so a silent numerical regression is reported as a win.

Detection signals: no CPU/reference comparison in the run log; benchmark numbers present but no max-error figure; index math changed alongside a padding or vectorization change without a re-run of the correctness harness.

Do not use: do not treat a speedup as success without a passing correctness comparison at the same commit. Do not apply padding or vectorization mechanically.

Safer fallback: capture a reference-comparison baseline first (skill:gpu:run-correctness-harness), re-run it after every index-math change, and only then compare benchmarks.
